message:
fix(data-pipeline): Convert GenBank 1-based coordinates to 0-based on parse

Fix coordinate system mismatch between GenBank format and internal representation:

GenBank format (NCBI standard):
- Uses 1-based coordinates
- Ranges are inclusive: "100..200" means positions 100 through 200

Internal representation:
- Uses 0-based coordinates
- Ranges are half-open [start, end): start at 0-based index, end exclusive

Coordinate conversion changes:

parseLocation() for ranges (e.g., "100..200"):
- start: parseInt(match[1]) - 1 → 0-based (100 becomes 99)
- end: parseInt(match[2]) → kept as-is (becomes exclusive upper bound)
- Math.max(0, ...) guards against underflow

parseLocation() for single positions (e.g., "100"):
- start: parseInt(match[1]) - 1 → 0-based
- end: start + 1 → exclusive end (single-bp feature has length 1)

parseLocation() for complement/join expressions:
- Same conversion applied to extracted coordinates

parseGenBank() qualifier flush:
- Save pending qualifier when transitioning from FEATURES to ORIGIN
- Fixes edge case where last feature's qualifier was lost

This ensures all gene coordinates in SQLite database use consistent
0-based half-open intervals, matching GeneMap rendering expectations.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
